prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: full-screen game world plate for an interactive railway operations ASMR simulator
Primary request: Create a polished dense isometric pixel-art game map of the Tokaido Shinkansen corridor in Japan, clearly inspired by late-1990s and early-2000s Japanese transport management games.
Scene/backdrop: a recognizable simplified central Japan rail corridor with Tokyo/Yokohama urban coast at the right/east edge, Nagoya dense city in the center, Kyoto temple district and Shin-Osaka urban rail yards at the left/west edge. Include ocean bays, rivers, bridges, green mountains, city blocks, elevated Shinkansen viaducts, station platforms, overhead gantries, parallel tracks, roads, canals, tiny boats, parks, and dense miniature buildings.
Subject: rail infrastructure and environment only; this is a board for interactive train sprites.
Style/medium: crisp 2D isometric pixel-art, A-Train / Japanese railway management sim / SimCity operations screen density, hand-painted pixel texture, readable miniature details, cohesive production-game art.
Composition/framing: wide 16:10 landscape, rail corridor flows horizontally across the middle with slight curves; urban districts and stations are distributed along the track; leave top, left, right, and bottom edges usable for HUD overlays.
Lighting/mood: calm sunny morning, cozy ASMR railway ambience, polished and readable.
Color palette: rich ocean blues, lush greens, concrete grays, muted city colors, warm amber platform lights, blue-white Shinkansen infrastructure accents.
Critical constraints: no foreground train, no giant train, no large train baked into background, no UI panels, no readable text, no labels, no logos, no watermark, no checkerboard. Tiny parked train hints in stations are acceptable only if very small and embedded.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: transparent in-game train sprite
Primary request: Create a single detailed N700S-inspired Shinkansen train sprite for an isometric pixel-art railway simulator.
Subject: white high-speed Shinkansen train with thin blue stripe, long segmented cars, rounded nose, roof panels, pantographs, dark windows, subtle pixel highlights. No logos.
Style/medium: crisp 2D isometric pixel-art sprite, late-1990s Japanese railway management game, readable at small size, polished.
Composition/framing: the train is isolated, angled from lower-left nose to upper-right tail, centered with generous padding; no track, no city, no shadow except a very subtle contact shadow if transparent-safe.
Lighting/mood: calm daylight, miniature game asset.
Constraints: transparent background, no checkerboard, no text, no logos, no watermark, no scene background, no platform, no rails. The entire train must be one cohesive sprite with clean edges.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: transparent in-game train sprite
Primary request: Create a single detailed N700A-inspired Shinkansen train sprite for an isometric pixel-art railway simulator.
Subject: white high-speed Shinkansen train with thin gold stripe, long segmented cars, rounded nose, roof panels, pantographs, dark windows, subtle pixel highlights. No logos.
Style/medium: crisp 2D isometric pixel-art sprite, late-1990s Japanese railway management game, readable at small size, polished.
Composition/framing: the train is isolated, angled from lower-left nose to upper-right tail, centered with generous padding; no track, no city, no shadow except a very subtle contact shadow if transparent-safe.
Lighting/mood: calm daylight, miniature game asset.
Constraints: transparent background, no checkerboard, no text, no logos, no watermark, no scene background, no platform, no rails. The entire train must be one cohesive sprite with clean edges.
